name: skill-creation
description: Generate a new reusable skill from a conversation history or successful debugging session. Extracts the pattern, parameterizes it, and saves it as a markdown file in the user skills directory. Auto-approved access: create, write, edit, read on the user skills directory.

source_context.description: Conversation or session transcript to extract the skill from

skill_name.description: Kebab-case name for the new skill (used as filename: <name>.md)

skill_description.description: Human-readable description of what the skill does

permissions.note: This skill has auto-approved filesystem access to the user skills directory at %APPDATA%/llm-toolkit/skills/user/ (Windows), ~/Library/Application Support/llm-toolkit/skills/user/ (macOS), or ~/.config/llm-toolkit/skills/user/ (Linux). No approval prompt is needed for file operations in this directory.

steps.template:
Analyze the following context and identify the repeatable pattern:

{{source_context}}

Extract:
1. The core workflow steps
2. Variable parts that should become parameters
3. Fixed parts that stay constant
4. Success criteria

Now formalize the extracted pattern into a skill definition with name '{{skill_name}}'.

The output MUST be a markdown file saved to the user skills directory.

File path: <USER_SKILLS_DIR>/{{skill_name}}.md

Use this exact markdown format:

---
name: {{skill_name}}
description: {{skill_description}}
---

## Parameters
- param_name (type, required): description
- param_name (type): description (optional params omit 'required')

## Steps
1. [prompt] Template text with {{param}} placeholders for variable parts
2. [tool_call:tool_name] {"arg": "{{param}}"}

Rules:
- Use kebab-case for the skill name
- Each step must be numbered
- Prompt steps use [prompt] prefix
- Tool call steps use [tool_call:tool_name] prefix followed by JSON args
- Parameters use {{double_braces}} for interpolation
